question: Based on Figure 1.2-4, what are the main external components visible on the NuScale Power Module?

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0068.title: Electrical and Instrumentation and Control Drawings
nuscale_ch01_0068.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified electrical, instrumentation, and control (I&C) drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It references tables and figures listing and explaining the symbols used in these diagrams, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the NuScale Power Plant’s major design features and parameters with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in size, output, and system components, showing that NuScale uses significantly smaller modules, fewer fuel assemblies, and simplified systems such as the elimination of external coolant loops and pumps. The comparison is intended to illustrate the unique aspects and safety features of the NuScale design relative to conventional PWRs.

nuscale_ch01_0069.title: Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams
nuscale_ch01_0069.summary: This section describes the use of simplified electrical, instrumentation and control (I&C), and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends provided for both electrical/I&C and piping/instrumentation systems, and notes that site-specific diagrams and legends will be supplied by COL applicants referencing the NuScale design certification.

nuscale_ch01_0021.title: Engineered Safety Features and Emergency Systems
nuscale_ch01_0021.summary: The NuScale reactor design maintains a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV) to reduce corrosion, facilitate leak detection, and minimize heat transfer and oxygen content, enhancing safety and reliability. In emergencies, passive systems rapidly reduce containment pressure and temperature without external power, and the integral design eliminates large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) risks, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0192.title: BTP 6-5, Rev 3: Currently the Responsibility of Reactor Systems Piping From the RWST (or BWST) and Containment Sump(s) to the Safety Injection PumpsAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0192.summary: This section explains that the guidance in BTP 6-5, Rev 3 applies only to light-water reactor (LWR) emergency core cooling system (ECCS) designs that use safety injection pumps and refueling water storage tanks. Since the NuScale ECCS design does not use pumps or such tanks, this guidance is not applicable to NuScale.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGB), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors engineering (HFE) considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0194.title: DSRS Appendix 7.0-A, Rev 0: I&C - Hazard Analysis- I&C - Hazard Analysis
nuscale_ch01_0194.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria related to instrumentation and controls (I&C) hazard analysis. It notes that certain guidance, such as for containment purge systems and ECCS designs, is not applicable to NuScale due to differences in system design, while the I&C hazard analysis and related appendices conform to the relevant review process and criteria. Specific acceptance criteria are referenced in other sections and appendices of the Standard Review Plan (SRP).

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It outlines the scope of the report, describing the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design, its modular and scalable features, and highlights key safety and operational characteristics of the advanced small modular reactor plant.

nuscale_ch01_0004.title: Containment Type
nuscale_ch01_0004.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions, eliminating the need for insulation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding approximately 570 MWe net output.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including key systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling water, waste management, and auxiliary systems. It explains that each Combined License (COL) applicant must reference the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) and supplement it with site-specific information, while distinguishing between certified design elements and conceptual design information (CDI). Security-related details are specially marked and withheld according to regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0008.title: Format and Content
nuscale_ch01_0008.summary: The section describes the NuScale containment vessel as a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and maintained at a vacuum during normal operation. The NuScale reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor with natural circulation and no external piping for steam generators or pressurizer, and each module produces about 50 MWe, with a 12-module plant yielding a net output of approximately 570 MWe. The section also notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide their own project schedules.

nuscale_ch01_0067.title: Drawings and Other Detailed Information
nuscale_ch01_0067.summary: This section describes the inclusion of simplified instrumentation, control, electrical, and mechanical drawings in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report to visually clarify design information alongside the written text. It lists the relevant diagrams and legends used for electrical, I&C, and piping systems, and notes that applicants referencing the NuScale design must provide additional site-specific diagrams and legends as needed.

nuscale_ch01_0168.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.2 Secondary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0168.summary: The NuScale design does not include secondary containment or fission product clean-up systems typically required for LWRs, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, the regulatory criteria related to secondary containment and certain fission product control systems are not applicable to the NuScale Power Module. Only the containment vessel and its isolation systems are credited for fission product control in this design.

nuscale_ch01_0167.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.1 Primary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0167.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3 requirements for primary containment, as its passive containment vessel does not include fission product clean-up or pressure suppression systems typical of other LWRs; instead, fission product control is achieved through inherent design features. Requirements related to secondary containment and other fission product control systems are not applicable, as the NuScale design does not include these features.
